← Terug naar Home

Milledoni - 1800 products

When you have over 1,800 products in a database, trying to render them all at once on a single webpage is obviously not ideal for performance.

The challenge was to display and load the products but not impact the UX and performance while doing so.

How I Did It

Instead of overwhelming the frontend, I decided to build server-side pagination. I configured the app to ask our Directus backend for only a bite-sized chunk of products at a time based on the URL.

  1. The Backend Fetch (+layout.server.js)
    First, I had to teach the server load function to be smart about what it fetched. I grabbed the page parameter right out of the URL, calculated the limit and offset, and passed that to the database.
// Grabbing the page number from the URL, defaulting to 1
const page = Number(url.searchParams.get('page')) || 1;
const limit = 20;
const offset = (page - 1) * limit;

// Fetching only what we need + the total count for the UI math
const [productsRes, totalRes] = await Promise.all([
  fetch(`YOUR_DIRECTUS_URL/items/products?limit=${limit}&offset=${offset}`),
  fetch(`YOUR_DIRECTUS_URL/items/products?aggregate[count]=*`)
]);

// ... extracting data ...

const totalProducts = totalData.data[0].count;
const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalProducts / limit);
  1. The Frontend UI (+page.svelte)
    With the backend serving neat, paginated plates of data, I updated the UI to consume it. I swapped out hardcoded numbers for dynamic counts and added load="lazy" to the product images for an extra performance boost.





  
{data.totalProducts} producten






  
Producten

  
    
    {#each data.product as product}
      
    {/each}

Beneath the list, I built a navigation bar with buttons that route the user to the correct /?page=X URL, triggering the server to fetch the next batch.

  1. The Polish & Micro-interactions
    Features aren't done until they feel good to use.

Smooth Scrolling: I added scroll-behavior: smooth; to the html tag in style.css. Now, when users click through pages, the browser glides nicely.

UX Tweaks: I added custom cursor states for active buttons to make the pagination controls feel tactile.

SEO: Added a proper Milledoni tag in app.html so the tab doesn't just say "Document".

What I Learned

Ultimately, this PR was a huge lesson in data flow. I learned how to preload only certain amount of data
